status_legend.active: The one feature currently being worked.
status_legend.blocked: Progress is stopped by a documented blocker.
status_legend.passing: Verification passed and evidence is recorded.

features.title: RunHaven project rename
features.user_visible_behavior: The repo, installable package, CLI command, image tags, runtime resource names, docs, and harness metadata consistently use the RunHaven name.
verification: Old project, module, and CLI names are absent from tracked text
Python package imports use `runhaven`
Console command is `runhaven`
Tests and package build pass
Harness audit passes
evidence: 2026-06-14: no-ignore old-name text scan across working tree files outside `.git` returned no matches

features.title: Repository harness operating layer
features.user_visible_behavior: A new agent can start from repo files, choose one scoped objective, run verification, and leave a clean handoff without relying on chat history.
verification: Root instructions route to state, startup, verification, and done criteria
macOS verification entrypoint runs the full local check set
Harness docs include manifest, component inventory, verification matrix, evidence log, security map, and lifecycle files
Harness audit passes the configured threshold
evidence: 2026-06-14: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed

features.title: Runtime hardening and macOS-only boundary
features.user_visible_behavior: RunHaven rejects unsafe command inputs by default, exposes explicit unsafe overrides, manages RunHaven state volumes, and only supports macOS 26+ verification.
verification: Unsafe image references, resource values, network modes, workspaces, root users, and root groups are rejected by tests
TTY and interactive behavior are controllable from the CLI
RunHaven state listing and prune behavior are tested
macOS-only entrypoints and CI are enforced
Full local verification and harness audit pass

features.title: Provider egress allowlisting
features.user_visible_behavior: Internet-enabled agent runs can be restricted to reviewed provider and package endpoints instead of unrestricted egress.
verification: Threat model updated
Provider network mode is integrated into normal runs
Smoke harness proves host allowlist proxy on an internal Apple container network
Provider allowlist behavior tested
Runtime smoke proves denied and allowed network paths
Docs state exact limitations
Blocked provider hosts are surfaced to the user
Allowed hosts resolving to unsafe local addresses are denied
Provider policy decisions are persisted for later review
Host allowlist decisions can be explained before rerun
Provider endpoint matrix backs bundled hosts and known explicit-review hosts
Blocked-host review groups run id, rule, count, reason, and suggested next action
Live smoke harness can test bundled provider hosts for a selected profile
Empty allowlist behavior is regression-tested for internet, internal, provider, and proxy policy paths
evidence: 2026-06-14: Rendered Apple DocC networking docs with Playwright and checked generated DocC JSON endpoints for `ContainerNetworkService`.
2026-06-14: Reviewed the complete user-supplied DocC snapshot: 1,022 rendered Markdown pages plus raw DocC JSON, zero fetch failures, and no exact hits for egress or allowlist control terms.
2026-06-14: Local Apple `container` 1.0.0 CLI help and command reference expose DNS selection, NAT networking, and host-only networks, but no domain egress allowlist flag.
2026-06-14: Added reserved `--network provider` mode that failed closed at that stage until provider egress enforcement existed.

features.notes: Default internet mode remains unrestricted egress. Provider mode is integrated for normal runs through a managed internal network and host allowlist proxy. Bundled provider hosts now come from the reviewed provider endpoint matrix. Broad path-sensitive, telemetry, update, reporting, and weakly sourced hosts remain explicit `--provider-host` review items. Provider policy decisions are logged for review, and blocked provider targets are grouped after runs with run id, counts, reasons, matched rules, and suggested next actions.

features.title: Host-side auth broker boundary
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven auth status` and `runhaven auth explain AGENT` describe the credential broker boundary without reading or printing secrets. Codex can opt into a host-side API-key broker with `--network provider --codex-api-key-broker-env NAME`.
verification: Static auth broker metadata exists for every bundled profile
Codex API-key broker reads a named host environment variable only during real runs
Codex broker injects temporary custom-provider config and a placeholder token into the guest without exposing the raw key
Codex broker only accepts provider-network clients and forwards Responses API create requests
Codex broker records secret-free decisions in `runhaven auth log`
Optional Codex broker smoke harness skips without a key and runs only with an explicit disposable key environment variable
`runhaven auth status` prints broker status without reading credential stores or environment values
`runhaven auth explain AGENT` prints profile-specific auth guidance without brokered secrets
JSON auth output is deterministic and secret-free
Docs state the Codex prototype status, remaining design-only provider status, trust boundary, non-goals, and future acceptance criteria
Source ledger records current provider auth references
evidence: 2026-06-15: Added `src/runhaven/auth_broker.py` with static per-profile broker metadata.
2026-06-15: Added `runhaven auth status` and `runhaven auth explain AGENT` with optional JSON output.
2026-06-15: Focused auth CLI tests passed and verified fake `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` values were not printed.
2026-06-15: Manual `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m runhaven auth status` and `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m runhaven auth explain codex` checks passed.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 79 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build.
2026-06-15: Added opt-in Codex API-key broker prototype, focused planner/proxy/CLI tests, and official Codex configuration source notes.
2026-06-15: Added secret-free Codex broker decision logging, `runhaven auth log`, and optional `scripts/codex_broker_smoke.py` live-smoke harness.
features.notes: Codex has an opt-in host-side API-key broker prototype. Other agent auth brokers remain design-only. Auth status/explain/log do not inspect credential stores or environment values. `plan` and `run --dry-run` do not read the named Codex API-key environment variable. Broker logs omit token values, request bodies, and environment variable names.

features.title: Secret-free run observability
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven runs list`, `runhaven runs show RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs log RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs diff RUN_ID`, and `runhaven runs stop RUN_ID` show recent actual agent runs and control active runs without exposing commands or secrets.
verification: Actual standard runs append a secret-free record to `runs.jsonl`
Provider runs append a run record that shares the run id with provider egress and auth broker logs
Run records summarize provider policy, auth broker, cleanup outcomes, and git workspace changes
`runhaven runs list` shows recent run summaries
`runhaven runs show RUN_ID` prints text and JSON for one run
`runhaven runs log RUN_ID` joins the run record with matching provider policy and auth broker entries
`runhaven runs diff RUN_ID` prints a live git diff only after recorded metadata still matches the live workspace
`runhaven runs stop RUN_ID` stops only an active RunHaven-owned named Apple container
Run records and active-run markers omit diffs, file contents, prompts, command lines, agent arguments, environment variable names, environment values, request bodies, and token values
evidence: 2026-06-15: Focused run-observability CLI tests passed for standard run logging, provider/auth/cleanup summaries, `runs list`, and `runs show --json`.
2026-06-15: CLI suite, ruff, and mypy passed after adding the run ledger.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 104 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build.
2026-06-15: Focused `runs log` CLI tests passed for joined text and JSON output without secrets.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 106 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding `runs log`.

2026-06-15: Focused git metadata tests first failed because run records had no git object, then passed after recording before and after `HEAD`, dirty state, changed count, and capped relative paths without file contents.
2026-06-15: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_cli` ran 42 tests and passed after adding git run metadata.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 108 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding git run metadata.
2026-06-15: Manual `runs show` and `runs log --json` reader smoke passed for a git metadata run record.

2026-06-15: Focused `runs diff` tests first failed because `diff` was not a valid subcommand; the mixed committed-and-dirty regression then failed until dirty diff assembly included committed changes. The final focused set passed for committed diffs, dirty live diffs, untracked dirty diffs, mixed committed-and-dirty diffs, unavailable metadata refusal, stale `HEAD` refusal, and dirty path-set refusal.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 115 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding `runs diff`.
2026-06-15: Manual `runs diff` smoke passed for a committed git metadata run record.

2026-06-15: Focused `runs stop` tests first failed because active markers, named containers, and the `stop` subcommand did not exist, then passed after adding active-run markers and Apple `container stop` routing.
2026-06-15: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` ran 120 tests and passed after adding `runs stop`.
2026-06-15: Manual `runs stop` smoke passed for a temporary active-run marker with mocked Apple `container stop`.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 120 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding `runs stop`.
features.notes: `runs list/show/log/diff/stop` are the first run dashboard and recovery slices. `runs diff` prints live git output on demand and does not store patches. `runs stop` works only for active runs with a current active marker. Future `runs attach` remains planned.
